basic_details.job_title: Indian Overseas Bank IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 750 Posts
basic_details.short_information: The table below summarizes the key details of the Indian Overseas Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 notification.

application_fee.details: The application fee for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 must be paid online between 07.07.2026 and 22.07.2026 through the BEEP portal only. The fee is non-refundable and cannot be adjusted against any other engagement process.

age_limit.details: The minimum age is 20 years and the maximum age is 28 years as on the cut-off date of 01.07.2026 for General and EWS category candidates, meaning the date of birth should fall between 01.07.1998 and 01.07.2006, both dates inclusive. Candidates registered under NAPS should be less than 34 years as on the cut-off date, after applicable relaxation. | Category Age Relaxation Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe 5 years Other Backward Classes (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years Persons with Benchmark Disabilities 10 years | Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC candidates is allowed on a cumulative basis with only one of the remaining categories of relaxation. To claim age relaxation, candidates must submit the requisite certificate at the time of personal interaction along with original certificates.

qualification.details: Candidates applying for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 must be Citizens of India. Merely applying, being shortlisted, or appearing for document verification and personal interaction does not guarantee an offer of engagement in the Bank. | Candidates must hold a Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a University recognized by the Government of India, or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government. The result must be declared on or before 31.07.2026. Candidates registered under the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) must have had their graduation result declared between 01.03.2022 and 31.07.2026, both dates inclusive, and must produce mark sheets and provisional or degree certificates when required. | The minimum age is 20 years and the maximum age is 28 years as on the cut-off date of 01.07.2026 for General and EWS category candidates, meaning the date of birth should fall between 01.07.1998 and 01.07.2006, both dates inclusive. Candidates registered under NAPS should be less than 34 years as on the cut-off date, after applicable relaxation. | Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC candidates is allowed on a cumulative basis with only one of the remaining categories of relaxation. To claim age relaxation, candidates must submit the requisite certificate at the time of personal interaction along with original certificates.

salary_details.details: Selected apprentices will be paid a monthly stipend for the one-year contract period, based on branch category. | For candidates selected under NATS, the Bank's share of the stipend after adjusting the Government subsidy is as follows. | The Government share of Rs. 6,150 will be directly credited to the apprentice's bank account through DBT mode. Apprentices are not eligible for any other allowances or benefits.

selection_process: Test Name Number of Questions Marks
Selection for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 will be based on an online examination
a test of the local language wherever applicable
and personal interaction if decided by the Bank. Satisfying the eligibility criteria alone does not guarantee a call for the online examination.
The total duration of the examination is 90 minutes. The examination will be conducted online using the candidate's own front camera-enabled desktop
and candidates must display the same ID proof uploaded on the apprenticeship portal. Candidates must score a minimum aggregate percentage as decided by the Bank
with a 5 percent relaxation available for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates. There is no minimum qualifying mark for individual subjects.
Candidates applying for a particular state must be proficient in reading

and understanding at least one local language of that state. This test is conducted during document verification after qualifying the written examination. Candidates who have studied the specified local language up to 10th or 12th standard are exempted from this test.
A state-category-wise waitlist will be maintained for one year from the date of the result or the next engagement exercise
how_to_apply: Candidates can apply online for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 from 07.07.2026 to 20.07.2026 by following the steps below.
Register on the NATS portal at https://nats.education.gov.in or the NAPS portal at https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in before applying.
Visit the "Career Opportunities" page on www.bfsissc.com or https://beep.bfsissc.com.
NATS-registered candidates should log in at https://nats.education.gov.in/student_type.php and apply under "Apply against advertised vacancies" for Indian Overseas Bank.
NAPS-registered candidates should visit https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/apprenticeship/opportunity, search "Indian Overseas Bank," click "View," and then "Apply for this Opportunity."
Provide the NATS/NAPS enrolment number while filling the online application form.
Apply for engagement from only one State/UT, preferably the home State/UT.
Pay the requisite application fee/intimation charges online between 07.07.2026 and 22.07.2026.
No other mode of application or payment will be accepted. Applications once submitted cannot be withdrawn, and fees once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.
important_instructions: Candidates should not have undergone apprenticeship earlier with IOB or any other organization, or been terminated from apprenticeship training due to their own fault. Candidates with one year or more of training or job experience after completing the educational qualification are not eligible. Apprentices are not treated as "employees" of the Bank and are not entitled to employee benefits. Apprentices are entitled to one day of casual leave per month, totaling 12 days per year, with a maximum of 4 days availed at a time. No Ex-servicemen or Disabled Ex-servicemen shall be engaged as apprentices, and there is no reservation for them in this process. The Bank has no obligation to offer regular employment during or after the apprenticeship period. Furnishing false, incorrect, or tampered information will lead to disqualification or termination, even after engagement. Any request for change of address, email ID, or document verification dates will not be entertained. Any dispute arising out of this recruitment is subject to the sole jurisdiction of courts situated at Chennai.

content_sections.heading: IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 Overview
content_sections.text: The table below summarizes the key details of the Indian Overseas Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 notification.

content_sections.heading: IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates must note the following important dates for the IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 application process.

rows.activity: Opening date of online application on NAPS, NATS and BEEP
Closing date of online application on NAPS and NATS
Payment of application fee on BEEP

content_sections.heading: IOB Apprentice Vacancy Details 2026-27 (State-wise)
content_sections.text: Indian Overseas Bank has proposed 750 apprentice vacancies distributed state-wise and union territory-wise, including backlog vacancies. The full category-wise and PwBD breakup is given below. SC stands for Scheduled Caste, ST for Scheduled Tribe, OBC for Other Backward Classes, EWS for Economically Weaker Section, and GEN for General/Unreserved category. Under PwBD, HI denotes Hearing Impaired, OC denotes Orthopedically Challenged, VI denotes Visually Impaired, and ID denotes Intellectual Disability. The above vacancies include backlog, and the number of seats is provisional and may vary according to the Bank's actual requirements.

content_sections.heading: IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 must be Citizens of India. Merely applying, being shortlisted, or appearing for document verification and personal interaction does not guarantee an offer of engagement in the Bank. Candidates must hold a Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a University recognized by the Government of India, or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government. The result must be declared on or before 31.07.2026. Candidates registered under the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) must have had their graduation result declared between 01.03.2022 and 31.07.2026, both dates inclusive, and must produce mark sheets and provisional or degree certificates when required. The minimum age is 20 years and the maximum age is 28 years as on the cut-off date of 01.07.2026 for General and EWS category candidates, meaning the date of birth should fall between 01.07.1998 and 01.07.2006, both dates inclusive. Candidates registered under NAPS should be less than 34 years as on the cut-off date, after applicable relaxation. Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC candidates is allowed on a cumulative basis with only one of the remaining categories of relaxation. To claim age relaxation, candidates must submit the requisite certificate at the time of personal interaction along with original certificates.

content_sections.heading: IOB Apprentice Stipend 2026-27
content_sections.text: Selected apprentices will be paid a monthly stipend for the one-year contract period, based on branch category. For candidates selected under NATS, the Bank's share of the stipend after adjusting the Government subsidy is as follows. The Government share of Rs. 6,150 will be directly credited to the apprentice's bank account through DBT mode. Apprentices are not eligible for any other allowances or benefits.

content_sections.heading: IOB Apprentice Application Fee 2026-27
content_sections.text: The application fee for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 must be paid online between 07.07.2026 and 22.07.2026 through the BEEP portal only. The fee is non-refundable and cannot be adjusted against any other engagement process.

content_sections.heading: IOB Apprentice Selection Process 2026-27
content_sections.text: Selection for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 will be based on an online examination, a test of the local language wherever applicable, and personal interaction if decided by the Bank. Satisfying the eligibility criteria alone does not guarantee a call for the online examination. The total duration of the examination is 90 minutes. The examination will be conducted online using the candidate's own front camera-enabled desktop, laptop, tablet, or smartphone, and candidates must display the same ID proof uploaded on the apprenticeship portal. Candidates must score a minimum aggregate percentage as decided by the Bank, with a 5 percent relaxation available for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates. There is no minimum qualifying mark for individual subjects. Candidates applying for a particular state must be proficient in reading, writing, speaking, and understanding at least one local language of that state. This test is conducted during document verification after qualifying the written examination. Candidates who have studied the specified local language up to 10th or 12th standard are exempted from this test. A state-category-wise waitlist will be maintained for one year from the date of the result or the next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for IOB Apprentice Document Verification
content_sections.text: Candidates shortlisted for document verification or personal interaction must produce the following documents in original along with self-attested photocopies. Printout of the valid communication for personal interaction or reporting Printout of the email from BFSI SSC containing submitted application data Proof of date of birth (birth certificate or SSLC/Std. X certificate with DOB) Photo identity proof such as Passport, Aadhaar/e-Aadhaar, PAN Card, Driving Licence, or Voter's Card Consolidated mark sheets and relevant educational qualification certificates Caste certificate in the prescribed format for SC/ST/OBC/EWS candidates Non-creamy layer OBC certificate issued on or after 01.04.2026, where applicable Disability certificate in the prescribed format for PwBD candidates Any other relevant documents supporting eligibility

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27
content_sections.text: Candidates can apply online for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 from 07.07.2026 to 20.07.2026 by following the steps below. Register on the NATS portal at https://nats.education.gov.in or the NAPS portal at https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in before applying. Visit the "Career Opportunities" page on www.bfsissc.com or https://beep.bfsissc.com. NATS-registered candidates should log in at https://nats.education.gov.in/student_type.php and apply under "Apply against advertised vacancies" for Indian Overseas Bank. NAPS-registered candidates should visit https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/apprenticeship/opportunity, search "Indian Overseas Bank," click "View," and then "Apply for this Opportunity." Provide the NATS/NAPS enrolment number while filling the online application form. Apply for engagement from only one State/UT, preferably the home State/UT. Pay the requisite application fee/intimation charges online between 07.07.2026 and 22.07.2026. No other mode of application or payment will be accepted. Applications once submitted cannot be withdrawn, and fees once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27
content_sections.text: Candidates should not have undergone apprenticeship earlier with IOB or any other organization, or been terminated from apprenticeship training due to their own fault. Candidates with one year or more of training or job experience after completing the educational qualification are not eligible. Apprentices are not treated as "employees" of the Bank and are not entitled to employee benefits. Apprentices are entitled to one day of casual leave per month, totaling 12 days per year, with a maximum of 4 days availed at a time. No Ex-servicemen or Disabled Ex-servicemen shall be engaged as apprentices, and there is no reservation for them in this process. The Bank has no obligation to offer regular employment during or after the apprenticeship period. Furnishing false, incorrect, or tampered information will lead to disqualification or termination, even after engagement. Any request for change of address, email ID, or document verification dates will not be entertained. Any dispute arising out of this recruitment is subject to the sole jurisdiction of courts situated at Chennai.

faq.question: Q1. Has Indian Overseas Bank released the Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 notification?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian Overseas Bank has released the Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 notification vide Advertisement No. HRDD/APPR/01/2026-27 dated 06.07.2026 for 750 vacancies.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: The online application closes on 20.07.2026 on the NAPS and NATS portals.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are there in IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: There are 750 apprentice vacancies distributed across states and union territories.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 20 and 28 years of age as on 01.07.2026, with age relaxation applicable for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the educational qualification required for IOB Apprentice Recruitment?
faq.answer: A degree in any discipline from a recognized university, with the result declared on or before 31.07.2026.

faq.question: Q6. What is the stipend for IOB apprentices?
faq.answer: The stipend ranges from Rs. 14,000 to Rs. 15,500 per month depending on the branch category (Metro, Urban, or Semi-Urban/Rural).

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: The fee is Rs. 236 for SC/ST/PwBD, Rs. 708 for GEN/OBC/EWS female candidates, and Rs. 944 for GEN/OBC/EWS male candidates, including GST.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for IOB Apprentice Recruitment?
faq.answer: Selection is based on an online written examination, a local language test, and personal interaction, if conducted.

faq.question: Q9. When is the IOB Apprentice online examination scheduled?
faq.answer: The tentative date for the online examination is 02.08.2026.

faq.question: Q10. Can a candidate apply for more than one state under IOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: No, a candidate can apply for engagement from only one State or UT, preferably their home State/UT.
